hero_summary: In practical Arabic explanations, Hijaz Ajami is treated as a branch of the Hijaz family, built on a distinct combination of Hijaz on Dukah (D) and Kurd on Husayni (A).
short_identity: A Hijaz-family branch built on Hijaz on Dukah (D), with Kurd on Husayni (A) as its clearest secondary jins.

quick_facts.common_start: It usually begins by stabilizing Hijaz on Dukah (D), then rises to Husayni (A) to reveal the Kurd branch
quick_facts.character: Hijaz in its trunk, but with a distinct upper color because of the Kurdish branch

sayr.summary: The practical sayr of Hijaz Ajami usually works as follows: it begins from Hijaz on Dukah (D) and clearly stabilizes both the tonic and the first jins. Then it rises toward Husayni (A), but instead of opening Bayati or another Hijaz there, it reveals Kurd on Husayni. That is where the true distinction of the maqam is formed. From there the line may extend to the jawab, before returning at the end to Dukah (D).
resting_tones: Dukah (D): the final tonic, because the trunk jins is Hijaz on this degree
Husayni (A): the most important secondary point of rest, because it is the place of the Kurd jins that distinguishes the maqam from nearby Hijaz branches
Muhayyar (upper D): a possible upper jawab summit when the maqam expands higher
motion_notes: Hijaz Ajami is not ordinary Hijaz, but a Hijaz maqam with a Kurdish branch on Husayni (A)
If the upper region becomes Bayati, the ear moves toward the more common Hijaz in this practical explanation
If the upper region becomes a second Hijaz, the sound moves toward Shahnaz or Hijazayn
Some analytical writings also read this maqam inside a broader logic of transformational relations between maqamat, not only as a memorized scale-pattern
common_path: Expand to Muhayyar (upper D) when needed

common_modulations: Kurd on Husayni (A) as the jins that distinguishes the maqam
If the upper region becomes Bayati, the maqam approaches Hijaz in this practical reading
If the upper region becomes a second Hijaz, it approaches Shahnaz or Hijazayn
Some explanations relate it structurally to what is called the Jewish Scale, but this does not replace its definition inside the Arabic modal framework itself

reference_recordings.title: At this stage there are no strongly documented Arabic repertoire examples
reference_recordings.note: What is available here is clearer in theoretical structure and practical differentiation from nearby branches than in a list of famous agreed-upon works
